pi_items.verified_value: Third-Party Risk Manager (specialization under Security Management Specialists)
pi_items.source: O*NET Online (https://www.onetonline.org/link/summary/13-1199.07) lists 'Security Management Specialists' with sample titles including Security Analyst, Security Consultant, Security Specialist. 'Third-Party Risk Manager' is a standard industry specialization within this category, widely used in banking/financial services for vendor risk oversight.

Confirmed via O*NET Online (https://www.onetonline.org/link/summary/13-1199.07) as the official SOC code for Security Management Specialists under Business Operations Specialists, All Other (13-1199).

pi_items.verified_value: Banking / Financial Services (NAICS 52: Finance and Insurance)
pi_items.source: NAICS classification system places banking under sector 52 (Finance and Insurance), subsector 522 (Credit Intermediation). Third-party risk management is a core regulatory requirement in banking under OCC Bulletin 2013-29 and FFIEC guidance.

Web search found a real 'First Continental Bank' (firstcontinentalbk.com) in Bradenton, FL, but it is a small institution (~1,100 employees, 27 branches) with no indication of being a major financial services bank. The task uses this as a fictional/synthetic organization context for the scenario. No bank matching 'First Continental Bank' with the described profile exists.

Web search found NovaCrest LLC (Atlanta, CPA/accounting firm), Novacrest Consulting, and Novacrest Solutions Limited (Lagos, Nigeria). No company called 'NovaCrest Technologies' exists. This is a synthetic vendor name created for the task scenario.

metadata_value: Vendor Lock-In and Service Continuity Risk
verified_value: Vendor Lock-In and Service Continuity Risk
pi_items.source: Task-defined creation targets. These are standard TPRM risk categories: data sovereignty is a recognized concern under EU-US Data Privacy Framework; vendor lock-in is a standard cloud risk per NIST SP 800-146; shared responsibility model gaps are documented by CSA Cloud Controls Matrix; fourth-party/subcontractor risk is addressed in OCC 2013-29. All are stored in the `third_party_risks` table (column: `title`, varchar 255). Verified via live DB schema inspection.

Verified against live Eramba DB table `risk_mitigation_strategies`: 1=Accept, 2=Avoid, 3=Mitigate, 4=Transfer. Query: SELECT id, name FROM risk_mitigation_strategies. Result confirmed in evidence file data_risk_strategy_mapping.txt. Mapping: Data Sovereignty=Mitigate(3), Vendor Lock-In=Transfer(4), Encryption Gap=Mitigate(3), Fourth-Party=Accept(1).

Task-defined creation targets. Stored in `security_services` table (column: `name`, varchar 255; column: `objective`, text). Schema verified via live DB: DESCRIBE security_services. The Internal Controls module is accessible at /security-services under Control Catalog sidebar menu. 2 pre-existing services confirmed: EDR and Vulnerability Management Program. Screenshot evidence: 08_internal_controls_security_services.png.

Task-defined creation target. Stored in `security_policies` table (column: `index` varchar 255 — NOTE: Eramba uses `index` not `title` for policy names). Schema verified via live DB. The Policies module is accessible at /security-policies under Control Catalog. 2 pre-existing policies confirmed: Acceptable Use Policy and Password Management Policy. Screenshot evidence: 09_policies_module.png.

Verified against live Eramba DB. Pre-seeded policies (Acceptable Use, Password Management) both have status=1. Confirmed from data_preseeded_policies.txt: both show status=1 and display as 'Approved' in the UI. Status 0 = Draft, Status 1 = Approved in Eramba's security_policies table.

Task-defined creation target. Stored in `projects` table (column: `title`, varchar 255). Schema verified via live DB. Projects module accessible at /projects under Security Operations > Project Management. Baseline count = 0 (empty at task start). Screenshot evidence: 06_projects_module.png.

Task-defined creation targets. Stored in `risk_exceptions` table (column: `title`, varchar 255). Schema verified via live DB. Risk Exceptions module accessible at /risk-exceptions under Risk Management. Baseline count = 0. Screenshot evidence: 05_risk_exceptions_module.png.

Task-defined dates. The risk_exceptions table stores expiry in the `expiration` column (type: date, NOT NULL). Column name verified via DESCRIBE risk_exceptions — it is `expiration`, NOT `review_planned_date` (which is used in a different table). Schema evidence: schema_risk_exceptions.txt. Export script correctly queries this column.

privileged_info_summary:
This task requires creating records across 6 Eramba Community v3.28.2 modules. Eramba credentials: admin / Admin2024! at http://localhost:8080. Credentials file is at /home/ga/eramba/credentials.txt and copied to Desktop.

Pre-seeded data (already exists at task start — agent must NOT duplicate these): 2 third parties (id=1 'AWS (Amazon Web Services)', id=2 'Salesforce Inc.'), 3 risks in the risks table (Phishing/no strategy, Ransomware/Mitigate, Insider Threat/Accept), 2 security services (id=1 'Vulnerability Management Program', id=2 'Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR)'), 2 policies (id=1 'Acceptable Use Policy' Approved, id=2 'Password Management Policy' Approved). There are 0 third-party risks, 0 projects, and 0 risk exceptions at baseline.

Required deliverables: (1) Third party 'NovaCrest Technologies' in Organization > Third Parties (/third-parties) with description mentioning IaaS, SOC 2, FedRAMP, Austin TX. (2) Four third-party risks in Risk Management > Third Party Risks (/third-party-risks): 'Data Sovereignty Compliance Risk' strategy Mitigate (DB id=3), 'Vendor Lock-In and Service Continuity Risk' strategy Transfer (DB id=4), 'Shared Responsibility Encryption Gap' strategy Mitigate (DB id=3), 'Fourth-Party Subcontractor Concentration Risk' strategy Accept (DB id=1). (3) Three internal controls in Control Catalog > Internal Controls (/security-services): 'Vendor Data Sovereignty Monitoring', 'Cloud Encryption Key Management Service', 'Fourth-Party Concentration Risk Assessment Program'. (4) Security policy 'Third-Party IaaS Security Requirements Policy' in Control Catalog > Policies (/security-policies) with status Approved (status=1) and description covering data residency, encryption key ownership, subcontractor disclosure, 24-hour incident notification SLA. NOTE: Eramba stores policy titles in the `index` column, not `title`. (5) Project 'NovaCrest Vendor Onboarding Q2 2025' in Security Operations > Project Management (/projects). (6) Two risk exceptions in Risk Management > Risk Exceptions (/risk-exceptions): 'NovaCrest Interim Operations Exception' expiry 2025-09-30 and 'NovaCrest Encryption Key Migration Exception' expiry 2025-12-31. NOTE: Eramba stores expiry in the `expiration` column.

CRITICAL: Online Assessments is Enterprise-only and NOT available in Eramba Community Edition. The task deliberately avoids this module. All 6 required modules (Third Parties, Third Party Risks, Internal Controls, Policies, Projects, Risk Exceptions) are confirmed working with visible '+ Add item' buttons in Community v3.28.2.

Navigation: Third Parties under Organization menu; Third Party Risks and Risk Exceptions under Risk Management menu; Internal Controls and Policies under Control Catalog menu; Projects under Security Operations > Project Management.
